# markgrab

> Universal web content extraction — URL to LLM-ready markdown

## Purpose

Extracts content from web pages, YouTube videos, PDFs, and DOCX files and converts them to clean, LLM-ready markdown. Auto-detects content type from URL and falls back to browser rendering for JavaScript-heavy pages when Playwright is installed.

## Capabilities

- **HTML extraction**: Fetches web pages via httpx, parses with BeautifulSoup, converts to markdown via markdownify
- **YouTube transcripts**: Extracts video transcripts using youtube-transcript-api with timestamped markdown output
- **PDF extraction**: Converts PDF documents to markdown via pdfplumber with page-level structure
- **DOCX extraction**: Converts Word documents to markdown via python-docx preserving heading hierarchy
- **Browser rendering**: Optional Playwright-based rendering for JS-heavy/SPA pages with auto-fallback
- **Anti-bot stealth**: Playwright stealth scripts (webdriver flag removal, plugin mocking, WebGL spoofing)
- **Content filtering**: Three-stage noise removal (noise tags, cookie/consent popups, hidden elements), density-based sidebar removal, link-density analysis
- **Output truncation**: Configurable max character limit (default 50,000) with smart line-boundary truncation
- **Proxy support**: Route requests through HTTP/SOCKS5 proxies
- **Language detection**: Character-frequency analysis for ko/ja/zh/en
- **Locale auto-detection**: Browser locale and timezone from URL TLD (.kr, .jp, .cn)
- **MCP server**: FastMCP-based server with `extract_url` and `extract_multiple` tools
- **CLI**: Single-command extraction with markdown/text/JSON output formats

## Installation

```bash
pip install markgrab                # core (httpx + beautifulsoup4 + markdownify)
pip install markgrab[browser]       # + Playwright
pip install markgrab[youtube]       # + YouTube transcripts
pip install markgrab[pdf]           # + PDF support
pip install markgrab[docx]          # + DOCX support
pip install markgrab[mcp]           # + FastMCP server
pip install markgrab[all]           # everything
```

Core dependencies: `httpx>=0.28`, `beautifulsoup4>=4.13`, `markdownify>=0.14`.
Requires Python >= 3.12.

## Quick Use

### CLI

```bash
markgrab https://example.com
markgrab https://example.com --format json --max-chars 10000
markgrab https://youtube.com/watch?v=... --format text
markgrab https://example.com --browser --proxy http://proxy:8080
```

### Python

```python
from markgrab import extract

result = await extract("https://example.com", max_chars=50_000)
print(result.title, result.markdown, result.word_count, result.language)
```

### MCP

Server name: `markgrab` (command: `markgrab-mcp`)
Tools: `extract_url`, `extract_multiple`

## Architecture

```
URL
 │
 ├─ _detect_type_from_url() ─────────────────────────┐
 │                                                    │
 │   youtube ───► YouTubeParser.parse()               │
 │   pdf/docx ──► _fetch_bytes() → PdfParser/DocxParser.parse()
 │   html ──────► Engine.fetch() → HtmlParser.parse() │
 │                    │                               │
 │        ┌───────────┴───────────┐                   │
 │    HttpEngine           BrowserEngine              │
 │   (httpx GET)        (Playwright headless)         │
 │        │                    │                      │
 │        └──── fallback ──────┘                      │
 │             (thin content < 50 words               │
 │              or HTTP error)                        │
 │                                                    ▼
 │                                            ExtractResult
 │                                                    │
 │                                          truncate_result()
 │                                                    │
 │                                              final result
 └────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Subpackages:
  markgrab/
  ├── core.py           # Main orchestrator: extract()
  ├── result.py         # ExtractResult dataclass
  ├── utils.py          # detect_language()
  ├── __main__.py       # CLI entry point
  ├── mcp_server.py     # FastMCP server
  ├── engine/
  │   ├── base.py       # Engine ABC, FetchResult, USER_AGENTS
  │   ├── http.py       # HttpEngine (httpx)
  │   └── browser.py    # BrowserEngine (Playwright)
  ├── parser/
  │   ├── base.py       # Parser ABC
  │   ├── html.py       # HtmlParser (BS4 + markdownify)
  │   ├── youtube.py    # YouTubeParser (youtube-transcript-api)
  │   ├── pdf.py        # PdfParser (pdfplumber)
  │   └── docx.py       # DocxParser (python-docx)
  ├── filter/
  │   ├── noise.py      # clean_soup() — tag/popup/hidden removal
  │   ├── density.py    # filter_low_density() — sidebar/nav removal
  │   └── truncate.py   # truncate_result() — max_chars enforcement
  └── anti_bot/
      └── stealth.py    # apply_stealth() — Playwright stealth scripts
```

HTML parsing pipeline: `clean_soup()` (noise removal) -> `_find_content()` (main content detection via semantic elements and CSS selectors) -> `filter_low_density()` (sidebar/nav removal) -> markdownify conversion.

Content detection priority: semantic element if focused (`<article>`, `<main>`, `[role='main']`) > CSS class/id selectors (30+ patterns) > semantic element if broad > `<body>` fallback.

## API Reference

### Top-level exports

```python
from markgrab import extract, ExtractResult
```

---

### `extract()`

```python
async def extract(
    url: str,
    *,
    engine: Engine | None = None,
    max_chars: int = 50_000,
    use_browser: bool = False,
    stealth: bool = False,
    timeout: float = 30.0,
    proxy: str | None = None,
    locale: str | None = None,
    browser_fallback: bool = True,
) -> ExtractResult
```

Main entry point. Routes URL to the appropriate engine and parser based on auto-detected content type.

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `url` | `str` | *(required)* | Target URL to extract content from |
| `engine` | `Engine \| None` | `None` | Custom engine instance. Default: HttpEngine with browser fallback |
| `max_chars` | `int` | `50_000` | Maximum characters for text and markdown output |
| `use_browser` | `bool` | `False` | Force Playwright browser rendering (raises ImportError if not installed) |
| `stealth` | `bool` | `False` | Apply anti-bot stealth scripts when using browser |
| `timeout` | `float` | `30.0` | Request timeout in seconds |
| `proxy` | `str \| None` | `None` | Proxy URL (e.g., `"http://proxy:8080"`, `"socks5://proxy:1080"`) |
| `locale` | `str \| None` | `None` | Browser locale. Default: auto-detect from URL TLD (e.g., `.kr` -> `ko-KR`) |
| `browser_fallback` | `bool` | `True` | Auto-fallback to browser on HTTP error or thin content (< 50 words). Set `False` for HTTP-only extraction |

**Returns:** `ExtractResult`

**Content type routing:**
- YouTube URLs (`youtube.com`, `youtu.be`) -> `YouTubeParser`
- `.pdf` URLs -> `PdfParser`
- `.docx` URLs -> `DocxParser`
- Everything else -> `HtmlParser` (with optional browser fallback)
- URLs returning `application/pdf` Content-Type header are also routed to `PdfParser`

---

### `ExtractResult`

```python
@dataclass
class ExtractResult:
    title: str
    text: str
    markdown: str
    word_count: int
    language: str
    content_type: str
    source_url: str
    metadata: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
```

**Fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `title` | `str` | Page title (from og:title, `<title>`, `<h1>`, or document properties) |
| `text` | `str` | Plain text content (newline-separated, whitespace-normalized) |
| `markdown` | `str` | Markdown-formatted content (ATX headings, `-` bullets) |
| `word_count` | `int` | Number of whitespace-delimited words in `text` |
| `language` | `str` | Detected language code: `"en"`, `"ko"`, `"ja"`, or `"zh"` |
| `content_type` | `str` | Content category: `"article"`, `"video"`, `"pdf"`, or `"document"` |
| `source_url` | `str` | Final URL after redirects |
| `metadata` | `dict` | Extra metadata (varies by content type, see below) |

**Metadata by content type:**

- **article** (HTML): `url`, `domain`, `description` (og:description), `image` (og:image), `author`, `published`
- **video** (YouTube): `video_id`, `transcript_language`
- **pdf**: `page_count`, `author`, `subject`, `created`
- **document** (DOCX): `author`, `created`, `modified`, `subject`

---

### Engine classes

#### `Engine` (abstract base)

```python
class Engine(ABC):
    def __init__(self, *, proxy: str | None = None): ...

    @abstractmethod
    async def fetch(self, url: str, *, timeout: float = 30.0) -> FetchResult: ...
```

Base class for content fetching engines. Subclass to create custom engines.

---

#### `FetchResult`

```python
@dataclass
class FetchResult:
    html: str
    status_code: int
    content_type: str
    final_url: str
```

**Fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `html` | `str` | Raw HTML content |
| `status_code` | `int` | HTTP status code |
| `content_type` | `str` | Content-Type header value |
| `final_url` | `str` | Final URL after redirects |

---

#### `HttpEngine`

```python
class HttpEngine(Engine):
    def __init__(self, *, proxy: str | None = None): ...
    async def fetch(self, url: str, *, timeout: float = 30.0) -> FetchResult: ...
```

Lightweight HTTP engine using httpx. Sends randomized User-Agent from a pool of 3 Chrome UA strings. Follows redirects. Raises `httpx.HTTPStatusError` on non-2xx responses.

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `proxy` | `str \| None` | `None` | Proxy URL |

---

#### `BrowserEngine`

```python
class BrowserEngine(Engine):
    def __init__(
        self,
        *,
        proxy: str | None = None,
        stealth: bool = False,
        locale: str | None = None,
    ): ...

    async def fetch(self, url: str, *, timeout: float = 30.0) -> FetchResult: ...
```

Playwright-based headless Chromium engine for JS-heavy and bot-protected sites. Requires `pip install markgrab[browser]`. Playwright is imported lazily.

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `proxy` | `str \| None` | `None` | Proxy URL |
| `stealth` | `bool` | `False` | Apply anti-bot stealth scripts |
| `locale` | `str \| None` | `None` | Browser locale. `None` = auto-detect from URL TLD |

**Behavior:**
- Viewport: 1920x1080
- Waits for `domcontentloaded`, then best-effort `networkidle` (max 8s or half timeout)
- CloudFlare/bot challenge retry: if page HTML < 20KB, retries up to 3 times with 2s delay
- Locale auto-detection: `.kr` -> `ko-KR`, `.jp` -> `ja-JP`, `.cn` -> `zh-CN`, default `en-US`
- Timezone set from locale: `ko-KR` -> `Asia/Seoul`, `ja-JP` -> `Asia/Tokyo`, `zh-CN` -> `Asia/Shanghai`, `en-US` -> `America/New_York`

---

### Parser classes

#### `Parser` (abstract base)

```python
class Parser(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    def parse(self, html: str, url: str) -> ExtractResult: ...
```

---

#### `HtmlParser`

```python
class HtmlParser(Parser):
    def parse(self, html: str, url: str) -> ExtractResult: ...
```

Parses HTML into `ExtractResult` using BeautifulSoup + markdownify.

**Pipeline:**
1. Extract title (priority: `og:title` > `<title>` > first `<h1>`)
2. Extract metadata (og:description, og:image, author, published date)
3. Remove noise elements via `clean_soup()`
4. Find main content area via semantic elements and 30+ CSS selectors
5. Remove low-density sidebars via `filter_low_density()`
6. Convert to markdown (ATX headings, `-` bullets) and plain text
7. Detect language

**Content detection CSS selectors** (tried in order after semantic elements):
`#contentBody`, `#powerbbsContent`, `#article-body`, `#mArticle`, `#postContent`, `#post-content`, `.view-content`, `.post-content`, `.article-content`, `.content-wrap`, `.entry-content`, `.article-body`, `.post-body`, `.board-content`, `.detail-content`, `.story-body`, `.article__body`, `.post__content`

---

#### `YouTubeParser`

```python
class YouTubeParser:
    def parse(self, video_id: str, url: str, title: str = "") -> ExtractResult: ...
```

Extracts YouTube video transcripts with timestamps.

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `video_id` | `str` | *(required)* | YouTube video ID (11 characters) |
| `url` | `str` | *(required)* | Original YouTube URL |
| `title` | `str` | `""` | Video title (fetched via oEmbed if not provided) |

**Language preference order:** `en`, `ko`, `ja`, `zh-Hans`, `zh-Hant`, `de`, `fr`, `es`, `pt`. Falls back to any available transcript.

**Markdown output format:**
```
# Video Title

[00:00] First transcript segment
[00:15] Second transcript segment
```

---

#### `PdfParser`

```python
class PdfParser:
    def parse(self, data: bytes, url: str) -> ExtractResult: ...
```

Extracts text from PDF bytes using pdfplumber.

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `data` | `bytes` | *(required)* | Raw PDF bytes |
| `url` | `str` | *(required)* | Source URL |

**Markdown output format:** Title from PDF metadata, then `## Page N` headings for multi-page documents.

---

#### `DocxParser`

```python
class DocxParser:
    def parse(self, data: bytes, url: str) -> ExtractResult: ...
```

Extracts text from DOCX bytes using python-docx.

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `data` | `bytes` | *(required)* | Raw DOCX bytes |
| `url` | `str` | *(required)* | Source URL |

**Heading mapping:** Word "Heading 1" -> `#`, "Heading 2" -> `##`, "Heading 3" -> `###`, other headings -> `####`. List styles -> `- ` prefix.

---

### Filter functions

#### `clean_soup()`

```python
def clean_soup(soup: BeautifulSoup) -> None
```

Remove noise elements from BeautifulSoup object in-place.

**Removes:**
- Tags: `script`, `style`, `noscript`, `svg`, `iframe`
- Cookie/consent/popup/modal elements (by class and id patterns)
- Hidden elements (`aria-hidden="true"`, `display: none`)

---

#### `filter_low_density()`

```python
def filter_low_density(content: Tag) -> None
```

Remove low-density sidebar/navigation blocks from content area in-place.

**Three-pass approach:**
1. Remove `<aside>` and `<nav>` tags (semantic non-content)
2. Remove elements matching sidebar class/id patterns (sidebar, related, widget, toc, breadcrumb, social, share, comment, advert, promo, recommend, popular, trending, signup, newsletter, subscribe)
3. Remove direct block children (`div`, `section`, `ul`, `ol`, `table`, `form`, `dl`) with link density > 50%

---

#### `truncate_result()`

```python
def truncate_result(result: ExtractResult, *, max_chars: int = 50_000) -> ExtractResult
```

Truncate text and markdown fields to `max_chars`. Breaks at the last newline before the limit and appends `[truncated]`. Returns the original result unchanged if no truncation needed.

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `result` | `ExtractResult` | *(required)* | Result to truncate |
| `max_chars` | `int` | `50_000` | Maximum character count for text and markdown fields |

---

### Anti-bot

#### `apply_stealth()`

```python
async def apply_stealth(context) -> None
```

Apply stealth settings to a Playwright browser context via `add_init_script`.

**Stealth measures:**
- Removes `navigator.webdriver` flag
- Sets realistic `navigator.languages` (`['en-US', 'en', 'ko']`)
- Mocks Chrome plugins (PDF Plugin, PDF Viewer, Native Client)
- Mocks `navigator.permissions.query` for notifications
- Adds `window.chrome` runtime mock
- Spoofs WebGL vendor/renderer to Intel Iris

---

### Utility functions

#### `detect_language()`

```python
def detect_language(text: str) -> str
```

Detect language from text using character frequency analysis on the first 2000 characters.

**Returns:** `"ko"` (Korean), `"ja"` (Japanese), `"zh"` (Chinese), or `"en"` (default).

**Detection threshold:** > 50 characters of the target script, or > 10% of the sample.

---

### MCP Tools

#### `extract_url`

```python
@mcp.tool()
async def extract_url(
    url: str,
    max_chars: int = 50_000,
    use_browser: bool = False,
    output_format: str = "markdown",
) -> str
```

MCP tool: extract content from a single URL.

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `url` | `str` | *(required)* | Target URL |
| `max_chars` | `int` | `50_000` | Maximum characters in output |
| `use_browser` | `bool` | `False` | Force Playwright browser rendering |
| `output_format` | `str` | `"markdown"` | `"markdown"`, `"text"`, or `"json"` |

**Returns:** String (markdown, plain text, or JSON depending on `output_format`).

---

#### `extract_multiple`

```python
@mcp.tool()
async def extract_multiple(
    urls: list[str],
    max_chars: int = 30_000,
) -> str
```

MCP tool: extract content from multiple URLs sequentially.

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `urls` | `list[str]` | *(required)* | List of URLs to extract |
| `max_chars` | `int` | `30_000` | Maximum characters per URL |

**Returns:** JSON array string. Each element: `{"url", "title", "word_count", "language", "markdown"}` on success, or `{"url", "error"}` on failure.

---

### CLI

```
markgrab [-h] [--max-chars MAX_CHARS] [--browser] [--timeout TIMEOUT]
         [--proxy PROXY] [--format {markdown,text,json}]
         url
```

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `url` | URL to extract content from |
| `--max-chars` | Max output characters (default: 50000) |
| `--browser` | Force Playwright browser rendering |
| `--timeout` | Request timeout in seconds (default: 30) |
| `--proxy` | Proxy URL (e.g., `http://proxy:8080`) |
| `--format`, `-f` | Output format: `markdown` (default), `text`, `json` |

**Output formats:**
- `markdown`: Prints `# Title` header with HTML comment metadata, then markdown body
- `text`: Prints `Title:` / `Words:` / `Language:` / `Type:` header, then plain text
- `json`: Prints JSON object with all `ExtractResult` fields

---

### Constants

```python
# markgrab.engine.base
USER_AGENTS: list[str]  # 3 Chrome 131 User-Agent strings (Linux, Windows, macOS)

# markgrab.core
_MIN_WORD_COUNT = 50  # Below this, content triggers browser fallback

# markgrab.filter.density
_LINK_DENSITY_THRESHOLD = 0.5  # Link text ratio above this = navigation
_MIN_BLOCK_TEXT = 25            # Minimum text length for density analysis

# markgrab.parser.youtube
_LANG_PRIORITY = ("en", "ko", "ja", "zh-Hans", "zh-Hant", "de", "fr", "es", "pt")
```

## Examples

### 1. Basic HTML extraction

```python
import asyncio
from markgrab import extract

async def main():
    result = await extract("https://example.com/blog/post")
    print(f"Title: {result.title}")
    print(f"Language: {result.language}")
    print(f"Words: {result.word_count}")
    print(f"Domain: {result.metadata.get('domain')}")
    print(result.markdown)

asyncio.run(main())
```

### 2. YouTube transcript extraction

```python
import asyncio
from markgrab import extract

async def main():
    result = await extract("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ")
    print(f"Title: {result.title}")
    print(f"Transcript language: {result.metadata['transcript_language']}")
    print(f"Content type: {result.content_type}")  # "video"
    # Markdown includes timestamps: [00:00] text, [00:15] text, ...
    print(result.markdown[:500])

asyncio.run(main())
```

### 3. Browser rendering with stealth for bot-protected sites

```python
import asyncio
from markgrab import extract

async def main():
    result = await extract(
        "https://protected-site.example.com/article",
        use_browser=True,
        stealth=True,
        timeout=60.0,
        max_chars=100_000,
    )
    print(result.markdown)

asyncio.run(main())
```

### 4. Custom engine with proxy

```python
import asyncio
from markgrab import extract
from markgrab.engine import HttpEngine

async def main():
    engine = HttpEngine(proxy="socks5://127.0.0.1:1080")
    result = await extract(
        "https://example.com",
        engine=engine,
        browser_fallback=False,  # HTTP-only, no browser fallback
    )
    print(result.title, result.word_count)

asyncio.run(main())
```

### 5. PDF extraction

```python
import asyncio
from markgrab import extract

async def main():
    result = await extract("https://example.com/report.pdf")
    print(f"Title: {result.title}")
    print(f"Pages: {result.metadata.get('page_count')}")
    print(f"Author: {result.metadata.get('author', 'unknown')}")
    print(result.markdown[:1000])

asyncio.run(main())
```

### 6. Batch extraction via MCP server

Configure in Claude Code's MCP settings:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "markgrab": {
      "command": "markgrab-mcp"
    }
  }
}
```

The server exposes two tools:
- `extract_url`: Single URL extraction with format options
- `extract_multiple`: Batch extraction returning JSON array

### 7. Using parsers directly for local content

```python
from markgrab.parser import HtmlParser, PdfParser

# Parse local HTML string
parser = HtmlParser()
result = parser.parse("<html><body><h1>Hello</h1><p>World</p></body></html>", url="local://")
print(result.markdown)  # "# Hello\n\nWorld"

# Parse PDF bytes
with open("document.pdf", "rb") as f:
    pdf_result = PdfParser().parse(f.read(), url="file://document.pdf")
print(pdf_result.title, pdf_result.metadata["page_count"])
```

## Links

- Docs: https://github.com/QuartzUnit/markgrab
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/markgrab/
- Source: https://github.com/QuartzUnit/markgrab
- Issues: https://github.com/QuartzUnit/markgrab/issues
